Martin: How are things going? In response to your question, there is no internal memo, but we do have copies of external law firm memoranda. Yes, ENA has changed its ISDA schedule form to provide for the "eligible contract participant" representation and line of business. I will verify with Susan Bailey as to whether these changes have been submitted to Ectric.

One of the most mortifying experiences of my career as a palliative care physician occurred a few years ago, when I was consulted to evaluate a retired English professor in her 70s, who was battling pancreatic cancer. Her symptoms were multifaceted, encompassing debilitating pain, persistent nausea, and intractable vomiting. As I engaged with her during our consultation, she inquired about the potential therapeutic benefits of medical marijuana in alleviating her distressing symptoms. I was taken aback, as my medical school education had not equipped me with any substantial knowledge on the subject, a glaring omission that left me feeling woefully unprepared to provide a well-informed response.

An electron is typically considered to have only one type of kinetic energy, but could it have more, related to its spin and charge, or by exciting other electrons? In one dimension (1D), the physics of interacting electrons is well described at low energies by the Tomonaga-Luttinger-Liquid (TLL) model, yet little has been observed experimentally beyond this linear regime. Here, we report on measurements of many-body modes in 1D gated wires using a tunneling spectroscopy technique. We observe two separate Fermi seas at high energies, associated with spin and charge excitations, along with the emergence of three additional 1D 'replica' modes that strengthen as the wire length decreases. The effective interaction strength in the wires is varied by altering the amount of 1D inter-subband screening by over 45%.

This paper shows that the eccentric debris rings seen around stars Fomalhaut and HD 202628 are narrower than expected in the standard eccentric planet perturbation scenario, sometimes referred to as "pericenter glow." The standard scenario posits an initially circular and narrow belt of planetesimals at semi-major axis $a$, whose eccentricity is increased to $e_f$ after the gas disc has dispersed due to secular perturbations from an eccentric planet, resulting in a belt of width $2ae_f$. In a minor modification of this scenario, narrower belts can arise if the planetesimals are initially eccentric, which could result from earlier planet perturbations during the gas-rich protoplanetary disc phase. However, a primordial eccentricity could alternatively be caused by instabilities that increase the disc eccentricity without the need for any planets.

Menstruation: A natural biological process that affects over 800 million women worldwide, yet it remains a taboo subject in many cultures. Despite its prevalence, it's common to hear whispers or hushed tones when discussing menstruation. As I speak to you now, millions of women are experiencing their periods, yet it's often treated as an embarrassing or shameful topic. For many, it's a source of discomfort, from the physical symptoms to the social stigma surrounding it. From my perspective and that of the women I've spoken to, it's frustrating to have to navigate through daily life with the added burden of hiding and discretion. It's exhausting to have to carefully conceal sanitary products in a brown paper bag and rush to the bathroom, trying to avoid drawing attention to oneself. The stigma surrounding menstruation can make it difficult to focus on anything else, even during important meetings or classes, as the discomfort and embarrassment can be all-consuming.

Throughout my time at Casa Pescatore, I have always striven to create a welcoming atmosphere for all visitors. I strive to make them feel as comfortable as they would in their own home. Yesterday, I believe I succeeded in this endeavor. A large group of people gathered on Perry Avenue with what some might consider "extreme prejudice." At 4:30 pm, my dear friend Eric and his son Nunu arrived with a delicious pan of baked ziti. The tables were set, and I had prepared a variety of dishes, including the baked ziti, for our guests. The sound of laughter and clinking silverware filled the air as we enjoyed each other's company. Just as I was about to take a moment to relax, my dear friend from Florida walked through the door. Seeing him brought a smile to my face, as I had missed him dearly. The added bonus was that he had brought his wife along, who is expecting and was eager to experience the warmth of our gathering. I spoiled her with tray after tray of delectable food and tiramisu, savoring every moment of our time together.

What is a parent? What is a parent? It's not an easy question. Today we have adoption, stepfamilies, surrogate mothers. Many parents face tough questions and tough decisions. Shall we tell our child about the sperm donation? If so, when? What words to use? Sperm donors are often referred to as "biological fathers," but should we really be using the word "father"? As a philosopher and social scientist, I have been studying these questions about the concept of parenthood. But today, I will talk to you about what I learned from talking to parents and children. I will show you that they know what matters most in a family, even though their family looks a little different.

Economy focus for election battle: Britain's economic future will be at the heart of Labour's poll campaign, Chancellor Gordon Brown has said. He was speaking after cabinet members held their last meeting at No. 10 before the expected election announcement. He said voters would recognise that Labour had brought stability and growth, and would continue to do so. Meanwhile, the Tories outlined their plans to tackle 'yob culture,' and the Lib Dems gave more details about their proposals to replace council tax. Earlier, the Archbishop of Canterbury wrote to all three parties, urging them not to fight the election by exploiting people's fears. In an open letter, he called on them not to turn the election into a competition about who can most effectively frighten voters about terrorism, asylum, and crime.

The UK's opposition Conservative Party has announced a proposal for mandatory health checks for immigrants if they secure victory in the upcoming general election. This initiative targets individuals looking to live and work in Britain from non-EU countries. Those intending to stay for six months or longer—and who are originating from regions with high tuberculosis rates—would be required to undergo a chest X-ray and additional tests as needed. Furthermore, all non-EU residents planning to remain for a year or more would be subject to a comprehensive medical examination for tuberculosis, hepatitis B, and HIV. A positive tuberculosis diagnosis would lead to an automatic rejection of visa applications, while other medical conditions would be evaluated on an individual basis.

"Being the third installment in the trilogy, I'll tackle the negative aspects first. While I appreciate the additional story provided by pre-quals, I dislike the approach as it feels like going backwards in the story rather than forwards. The combat also feels sloppy and doesn't have the same sharpness as Arkham City. As of the writing of this recommendation (October 2013), a game-breaking bug is preventing a side mission from being completed. Some gadgets are assigned to ridiculous hotkeys, making it difficult to access them in combat. While these issues may seem minor, they can have a significant impact on the overall experience.

I've attached the FERC order and staff recommendations from the Skean.nsf file. The order outlines the possibility of $69 million in refunds, requiring either payment or cost justification. Although Enron is not listed among the 13 entities accused of overcharging, PGE is, with an allegation of $3.18 million. This is not the end of the matter. The order currently addresses only January 2001 and indicates that further directives will cover December 2000 and establish benchmark prices through April 2001. The pricing used to determine potential refunds was quite high, at $273/MWh, incorporating gas prices, emissions costs, and an assumed heat rate exceeding 16,000. Regarding sales to California, we have finalized at least one short-term transaction and held discussions with the California Department of Water Resources about longer-term agreements. However, the last update indicated that we were still encountering issues with credit.

We present a groundbreaking Skyrme-like conserved current within the effective theory framework of pions and vector mesons, formulated through the lens of hidden local symmetry. Remarkably, the corresponding charge coincides precisely with the Skyrmion charge for all smooth field configurations. Furthermore, our analysis reveals the existence of singular configurations that can be interpreted as n_f=1 baryons carrying charge under this novel symmetry. In this context, vector mesons naturally assume the role of Chern-Simons vector fields residing on the quantum Hall droplet that constitutes the n_f=1 baryon. We advance the compelling hypothesis that this current represents the proper low-energy effective description of the baryon current. This perspective offers a unified framework for understanding both baryon types while enabling their seamless transformation into one another. Additionally, we demonstrate that Chern-Simons dualities emerging on the droplet can be understood as manifestations of a Seiberg-like duality between gluons and vector mesons.

The comedy *Sideways* triumphed at this year's Independent Spirit Awards, sweeping all six categories for which it was nominated. The film claimed Best Feature, while Alexander Payne earned Best Director and Best Screenplay, sharing the latter honor with co-writer Jim Taylor. The cast also shone, with Paul Giamatti, Thomas Haden Church, and Virginia Madsen each securing acting awards. *Sideways*, a strong contender at Sunday’s Oscars with five nominations, continued its award-season momentum.  

Now in their 20th year, the Spirit Awards celebrate films produced outside the traditional studio system and are traditionally held the day before the Oscars. Among other winners, Catalina Sandino Moreno claimed Best Actress for her portrayal of a drug smuggler in the Colombian drama *Maria Full of Grace*—a role that also earned her an Oscar nomination. Joshua Marston received the Best First Screenplay award for the same film.

Continuous-time quantum walks serve as a powerful paradigm for developing numerous quantum algorithms. A key performance metric in this framework is the quantum hitting time—the duration needed for the quantum walk to locate a target vertex with high probability. This work presents enhanced upper bounds for quantum hitting times, applicable to a broad class of continuous-time quantum walk algorithms. Specifically, we demonstrate these improvements through the glued-trees problem, achieving a polynomial reduction in the hitting time bound from $o(n^5)$ to $o(n^2\log n)$. Additionally, our techniques yield an exponential improvement in precision dependence for the continuous-time quantum walk algorithm by Chakraborty et al., which identifies marked nodes in ergodic, reversible Markov chains.

In 1903, the President of the United States embarked on a three-day camping excursion in California's Yosemite Valley. President Theodore Roosevelt spent his nights amidst the majestic sequoia trees, camped through a snowstorm, and engaged in lengthy discussions by the campfire with his host and guide, the renowned conservationist John Muir. While Roosevelt was well known for his love of the outdoors, Muir had extended the invitation for deeper reasons: Yosemite was under threat. Although the area had been designated as protected land in 1864, by 1903, it still faced the risk of overdevelopment. This was part of a long-standing conflict over the preservation and public use of land—two objectives that proved challenging to achieve. The struggle for Yosemite's protection took root with the 1849 Gold Rush, as miners flocked westward in pursuit of gold in the Sierra Nevada foothills.

In this study, we use simulations to create a mock data set for the cosmic shear power spectrum and its covariance matrix, which are important for upcoming weak lensing surveys like Euclid or LSST. We correct these quantities for baryonic effects using a new method called the baryonification method, which was developed by Schneider et al. (2019). This method takes into account the effects of baryons on the distribution of matter in the universe.

We create a mock data set that mimics the properties of the cosmic shear power spectrum and its covariance matrix, which are crucial for understanding the distribution of matter in the universe. We then use this mock data set to infer the effects of baryons on the cosmic shear power spectrum and its covariance matrix.

Our results show that baryonic feedback effects can significantly suppress the power of the cosmic shear power spectrum on small scales. This suppression is a major source of uncertainty for upcoming weak lensing surveys, as it can affect their ability to measure the properties of the universe. By correcting for these effects

Attitude: bored. Mood: indifferent, but looking forward to tonight; it's been a long, hard day in the trenches here, folks. Almost no time to write anything for you readers. However, it's good to see Tigger (now Akcron) taking up the reins herself, making an appearance, and talking about things :) Not much to say about things; yesterday was Shakespeare on the Green. It was a lovely play, but I had to leave before the closing curtain, which was a bit disappointing. Hopefully, someday I'll see the entirety of a Shakespeare play. But I digress. Akcron is right about the problems with the shower—it’s not great. In terms of laughing at her in the shower, you have to admit, the look people get when committing self-torture can be pretty entertaining.

The economy will be at the heart of the election campaign, Chancellor Gordon Brown said. Speaking after a cabinet meeting at No 10 Downing Street, he stated that voters will recognize that Labour has brought stability and growth, and will continue to do so. The parties have outlined their plans to tackle 'yob culture' and the Liberal Democrats have provided more details on their proposals to replace council tax. Earlier, the Archbishop of Canterbury wrote to all three parties, urging them not to turn the election into a competition about who can most effectively frighten voters about terrorism, asylum, and crime. He called on them not to turn the election into a competition about who can most effectively frighten voters about these issues.

A critical examination of the previously outlined scenario reveals two significant concerns. Firstly, while it is true that the economic stimulus package has been implemented, its methodology is fundamentally flawed. As Robert Rubin astutely pointed out in a recent interview with NPR's Marketplace, the administration could have achieved the same level of economic stimulation without incurring substantial long-term debt by opting for temporary, targeted tax cuts that would have disproportionately benefited low- and middle-income individuals. The wisdom and expertise of Robert Rubin are a breath of fresh air, and his presence is a stark reminder of the dearth of similarly esteemed individuals within the current administration's domestic policy team. Secondly, it is imperative to recognize that granting the Republicans unchecked control during wartime is no longer a viable option, given their ingenious yet troubling ability to perpetuate a state of endless, global conflict under the guise of the 'war on terrorism.'

According to a UK think-tank report, a quarter of broadband users in Britain regularly upload content and maintain personal websites, with more than five million households in the UK having broadband connections and that number growing rapidly. The report highlights how having an always-on, fast internet connection is transforming the way people use the internet. More than half of those with broadband log on to the web before breakfast, while one in five admit to getting up in the middle of the night to browse the web. Significantly, the report suggests that broadband is empowering people to take a more active role online.

World leaders gather to face uncertainty as more than 2,000 business and political leaders from around the globe arrive in the Swiss mountain resort of Davos for the annual World Economic Forum (WEF). For five days, they will discuss issues ranging from China's economic power to Iraq's future after this Sunday's elections. UK Prime Minister Tony Blair and South African President Thabo Mbeki are among the over 20 government leaders and heads of state attending the meeting. Unlike previous years, protests against the WEF are expected to be muted, with anti-globalisation campaigners having called off a demonstration planned for the weekend. Meanwhile, the Brazilian city of Porto Alegre will host the rival World Social Forum, timed to run in parallel with the WEF's event in Davos.

Ireland surged past Scotland, maintaining their Six Nations Grand Slam ambitions with an impressive victory at Murrayfield. Hugo Southwell's try gave the Scots an early 8-0 lead, but scores from locks Malcolm O'Kelly and Paul O'Connell put the visitors in command by half-time. A third try from wing Denis Hickie and a third penalty from Ronan O'Gara, who kicked 13 points, extended the lead. Jon Petrie scored a second try for Scotland, but late scores from John Hayes and Gavin Duffy sealed the victory. Following two hard-earned away victories, Eddie O'Sullivan's side can now look forward to hosting England at Lansdowne Road in a fortnight. Scotland will attempt to give their coach Matt Williams his first Six Nations victory when Italy visit Edinburgh, but they again struggled to convert pressure into points.

Magic fans, beware... Wizards is afraid to make a decent digital game. At its core, Magic: The Gathering is a social game—one meant to be enjoyed with friends (or frenemies), laughing, talking smack, and dropping game-winning bombs. Wizards of the Coast seems to have forgotten this. If you choose to play with friends, you can't earn gold, and gold is essential. It's so important that they've implemented a daily cap, which is understandable since gold unlocks everything in-game. But prepare for a long and tedious 1v1 grind against the computer or random opponents, whom you can't even chat with. If you choose to play for free, expect to spend roughly four hours a day, alone, for over a month just to unlock the cards.

Last night, I had a particularly frustrating experience with my knitting project. I regret to admit that I discarded the sweater I was working on, as it had reached only about 14 inches in length. The yarn, which was worth approximately $10, had become increasingly knotted and tangled, making it difficult to frogging (ripping out rows) and salvage the project. In fact, the yarn broke frequently, which was quite vexing. I must admit that I didn't even curse, which is unusual for me, as I am not one to give in to frustration easily. Instead, I opted to take a break and indulge in a different activity - watching season two of Sex and the City, which I found quite engaging. However, I eventually grew tired of it and, in a moment of exasperation, I discarded the sweater. Despite the setback, I suppose I did learn a valuable lesson from the experience and will approach my next project with a more patient and methodical approach.

A groundbreaking report from University College London reveals that slashing the drink-drive limit by a third could save a staggering 65 lives annually, far exceeding initial estimates. The proposed reduction from 80mg of alcohol per 100ml of blood to 50mg would not only save lives but also lead to a significant decrease in serious injuries on the UK's roads. According to the research conducted by transport expert Professor Richard Allsop, the move would result in 230 fewer serious injuries compared to 2003. This reduction in road accidents would have a substantial economic impact, saving the country an estimated £119 million annually. A previous estimate, contained in a 1998 Department of Transport document, had previously suggested that such a move could save 50 lives a year, highlighting the significance of this new research. As MPs prepare to vote on the government's road safety bill, which includes changes to speeding fines but not the drink-drive limit, this study serves as a timely reminder of the importance of evidence-based policy-making.

Two twin domes, two radically opposed design cultures. One is made of thousands of steel parts, the other of a single silk thread. One is synthetic, the other organic. One is imposed on the environment, the other creates it. One is designed for nature, the other is designed by her. Michelangelo said that when he looked at raw marble, he saw a figure struggling to be free. The chisel was Michelangelo's only tool. But living things are not chiseled. They grow. And in our smallest units of life, our cells, we carry all the information required for every other cell to function and to replicate. Tools also have consequences. At least since the Industrial Revolution, the world of design has been dominated by the rigors of manufacturing and mass production.

I would like everyone to think before they act. Actually, I'd like each and every single person in the world to think before they get out of bed in the morning, and make a commitment to keep thinking all day, and if they're not prepared to, I'd like very much that they'd hop the heck back in bed, and drift off to an eternal slumber. I'm sick to death of stupid people. No, I'm not the smartest person in the world... But I'm not talking about things that take a lot of brain power, so I think it's fair to criticize the inanely stupid. Let's see if I can keep this particular rant to a few particular types, and we'll save some for another day, sound good? Great.

I think this Assassin's Creed nailed the mechanics on all fronts, including ship battles, sailing, hunting, parkour, and more. I felt a stronger attachment to this character as well. Although the story starts out rough, it significantly improves as you progress, which I think is a common trend in the AC series. Edward was an interesting character, and the side characters were equally engaging. I found that the collectibles in this game had a greater purpose and meaning, which I hadn't experienced in previous AC games. While they weren't required, they were enjoyable to collect and provided a balanced reward system.

You've been accused of a crime you didn't commit, and it's impossible to prove your innocence. If you insist on maintaining your innocence, you'll likely be found guilty and executed. However, if you confess, apologize, and implicate others, you'll be set free. This was the daunting choice facing those accused of witchcraft in Salem, Massachusetts, between February 1692 and May 1693. They were the victims of a mix of paranoia about the supernatural, misdirected religious fervor, and a justice system that prioritized repentance over truth. Salem was settled in 1626 by the Puritans, a group of English Protestants. Life was strict and isolated for the people of Salem, marked by frequent battles with their Native American neighbors and rival groups of French settlers.

I was negatively biased towards this game before I played it. Wasn't even sure about buying it. I don't know, the whole portal gun and portals thing seemed weird to me, even though I like science fiction. Boy, was I wrong. The jokes and voice acting in this game are phenomenal - just amazing. It's not cheesy (okay, it's sometimes cheesy). And different story characters have their own sense of humor. Those different characters really have distinct personalities. And the level design is just rad. By the way, I never played the first Portal game. One of these days, I'm planning to if I can spare the time. I would never have played it if I hadn't played the sequel.

Prince was crowned the 'top music earner' in 2004, outperforming other pop stars such as Madonna and Elton John in US Magazine's annual list. The singer generated a whopping $56.5 million (£30.4 million) from concerts, album, and publishing sales with his Musicology Tour and album. He narrowly edged out Madonna, who earned $54.9 million (£29.5 million) during her global re-invention tour. Veteran duo Simon and Garfunkel placed 10th, with their comeback tour contributing to their earnings of $24.9 million (£13.4 million) in 2004. Prince's successful return to the stage after a decade in the commercial wilderness was noted by the magazine, with his 2004 tour grossing $90.3 million (£48.5 million) in ticket sales and selling 1.9 million copies of his latest album, Musicology.

Electric network frequency (ENF) fluctuations serve as a powerful tool in multimedia forensics. An efficient approach for ENF estimation is introduced using temporal windowing based on the filter-bank Capon spectral estimator. A type of Gohberg-Semencul factorization of the model covariance matrix is employed due to the Toeplitz structure of the covariance matrix. Additionally, this approach uses, for the first time in the field of ENF, a temporal window—not necessarily rectangular—at the stage preceding spectral estimation. Krylov matrices are utilized for the fast implementation of matrix inversions. The proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in ENF estimation when a short time window of 1 second is used in power recordings. In speech recordings, the proposed approach yields highly accurate results regarding both time complexity and accuracy.

Establishing a comprehensive weather service for water is imperative, but it will only become a reality when we collectively demand accountability and drive the necessary investment. My journey to address this issue began eight years ago, when I first took the stage at TED, fresh out of graduate school. At the time, I was unaware that my brief presentation would set the tone for the questions I would be grappling with for the next decade. Like many young idealists, I was convinced that I could single-handedly solve the world's water problems through technological innovation. However, I soon discovered that the reality was far more complex. I had initially been misled into believing that the persistence of water quality issues was primarily due to the difficulty in identifying them. I assumed that the solution lay in developing simpler, faster, and more affordable sensors. This assumption proved to be misguided, and I have since come to realize that the challenges we face are far more nuanced.

Last night was a disaster. I ended up discarding the sweater I was knitting, which was about 14 inches long. I likely lost around $10 worth of yarn, considering it costs $8 per skein. Unfortunately, the project had become unmanageable by the time I reached the armholes, with too many stitches and the mohair constantly knotting itself together as I knitted. As a result, frogging didn't work well, and the yarn kept breaking. Surprisingly, I didn't even swear, which is out of character for me. Instead, I morosely ripped out the rows while listening to season two of Sex and the City, courtesy of the library. Eventually, I grew tired of it and threw the sweater away. I suppose I can say that I gained valuable experience from attempting to make this sweater.

Mutation-based fuzzing typically employs an initial set of non-crashing seed inputs, drawn from a corpus of diverse seeds, to generate new inputs by mutation. A comprehensive corpus of potential seeds often comprises thousands of similar inputs. However, the lack of diversity in this seed set can result in wasted fuzzing efforts, as exhaustive mutation from all available seeds may be unnecessary. To address this issue, fuzzers come equipped with distillation tools, such as afl-cmin, which select the smallest subset of seeds that trigger the same range of instrumentation data points as the full corpus. The common practice suggests that minimizing the number and cumulative size of the seeds leads to a more efficient fuzzing process, which we systematically investigate. Our findings are based on 34+ cpu-years of fuzzing with five distillation approaches, which enable us to understand their impact in finding bugs in real-world software.

Kindness of strangers... Attempted laundry today with the kids in tow and realized how quickly the simplest things can become a frustrating ordeal. I also learned how remarkably sweet total strangers can be for no reason other than because they feel like being kind. Trust me, it wasn't like they were trying to get into my pants or anything. I had kid drool all over me and was wearing my 'wash-day' outfit... Also, I'm not sure I'll ever be bathing again, so it's not like there was even an olfactory incentive there! I am currently composing a post and simultaneously watching the collected No Doubt videos. I am doing both of these things sitting at my desk in my room.

Hands down, my favorite first-person shooter of all time. The need for run-and-gun rambos is a thing of the past. Recoil and ballistics are remarkably realistic. Single hits to the chest, neck, or head from rifle-caliber weapons almost always result in death unless it merely grazes you. Rounds drop over distance and short, controlled bursts are essential if you wish to maintain a steady aim. There is no magical ammunition pool. If you reload halfway through your magazine, you'll find yourself with a pouch full of half-full mags. You can't sustain a sprint indefinitely. If you sprint for a while, your aim will suffer as you gasp heavily until you've caught your breath. Rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a windowsill to steady your aim. Accuracy suffers when standing, as the human body is not designed for sustained, prolonged periods of rapid movement.

In line with the findings from the Monte Carlo simulations as noted in Jiménez's study (J. of Turbul., 2020), dipoles play a crucial role in the dynamics of decaying two-dimensional turbulence, comparable to that of individual vortex cores. The kinetic energy in this turbulent flow primarily resides in elongated streams composed of interconnected dipoles. The vortices divide into two distinct groups: one consisting of small, fast-moving cores, and another of larger, slower ones, which appear to be 'frozen' into a gradually developing 'crystal.' The motion of these two groups is markedly different, with only the smaller, fast-moving group displaying self-similarity. The majority of the kinetic energy is attributed to the slower, larger vortices, which form the dipoles and streams. The text also explores the processes contributing to the expansion of this slow-moving component.

I have entered the volume and pricing details for the following two deals in Sitara: Deal 418015 for CNG citygate with Tenn production plus transport pricing, and Deal 418065 for CGas citygate with TRCO and CGLF production pricing, alongside TRCO, CGLF, and CGas transport pricing (please note that this process may take some time to complete). Should we consider disabling the auto-confirmations? It seems that whenever I create a VNG deal, a confirmation is automatically faxed to the parties involved. If anyone has a more efficient method for handling these deals—perhaps by categorizing them as production area sales—please share your ideas. The simplest approach I can suggest is to record the sales at the citygate or storage facility and handle most of the pricing via a spreadsheet.

Deep hashing techniques have demonstrated exceptional accuracy and efficiency in large-scale image retrieval. A central challenge in these methods is optimizing discrete hash bits. Typically, this involves using an activation function, such as the sigmoid or tanh, and minimizing a quantization loss to approximate discrete values. Unfortunately, this approach can cause hash bits to become stuck in the wrong saturated zones of these activation functions, creating what we refer to as the "dead bits problem" (DBP). Furthermore, the current quantization loss exacerbates the DBP. In this paper, we introduce a straightforward yet effective gradient amplifier, applied before the activation functions, to mitigate the DBP.

So, I graduated seven years ago from Berkeley with a dual degree in molecular and cell biology and linguistics. I attended a career fair on campus, where I landed an interview with a start-up called Theranos. At the time, there wasn't much information available about the company, but the little that was there was quite impressive. Essentially, Theranos was creating a medical device that would enable you to run your entire blood panel on a finger-stick of blood. This would eliminate the need for a large needle in your arm to obtain your blood test results. This was intriguing not only because it was less painful, but also because it had the potential to revolutionize diagnostic testing.

Do you ever have one of those moments when you begin to reflect on life? Like when you're sitting in math class, feeling bored out of your skull, and wondering what exactly goes on in your brain that makes you understand math? And then you realize that you exist and you're in math class, and you wonder why. Suddenly, it hits you  the fact that you're going off on this random train of thought is incredibly pathetic. Have you ever had that happen? I hate that. You know what else I hate? Block schedules. Who would ever come up with that idea? Anyone can see that sitting through a two-and-a-half-hour class in any subject is cruel and an unusual punishment.

02/07/2014 - It's a shame I can't thoroughly recommend or enjoy this game, as from the short time I've played it, I know I'd be addicted if it didn't have the following two game breakers. No Aussie servers - one red bar puts you at a severe disadvantage, creating unbearable lag. Mind you, I played through the offline training mode, and it was stuttering and lagging as well (how does that even happen?). Combine that with the poor ping, and it just ruins the game. Everything artifacts like crazy - the menus, backgrounds, training, and in-game. I've never had this happen before with this card and thought it might not like my overclock. Set my GPU back to stock, and sure enough, it didn't make a difference.

I have two questions for you. One: whose shoulders do you stand on? And two: what do you stand for? These are two questions that I always begin my poetry workshops with students because, at times, poetry can seem like this dead art form for old white men who just seem like they were born to be old, like, you know, Benjamin Button or something. And I ask my students these two questions, and then I share how I answer them, which is in these three sentences that go: I am the daughter of Black writers, who are descended from freedom fighters who broke their chains and changed the world. They call me. And these are words I repeat in a mantra before every single poetry performance. In fact, I was doing it in the corner over there. I was making faces.

Within general relativity, the unique stationary solution for an isolated black hole is the Kerr spacetime, characterized by a distinctive multipolar structure determined solely by its mass and spin. We have developed a general method to extract the multipole moments of arbitrary stationary spacetimes and have applied it to a broad class of horizonless microstate geometries. These geometries can disrupt the axial and equatorial symmetry of the Kerr metric, resulting in a much more complex multipolar structure, offering a pathway to constrain fuzzball models phenomenologically. We find numerical evidence indicating that all multipole moments are typically larger (in absolute value) than those of a Kerr black hole with the same mass and spin. Current measurements of the quadrupole moment of black-hole candidates can impose only mild constraints on fuzzballs, while future gravitational-wave detections of extreme mass-ratio inspirals with the LISA space mission will significantly improve these bounds.

The direpack package aims to provide a set of modern statistical dimension reduction techniques as a single, consistent package in Python. The included methods fall into three categories: projection pursuit-based dimension reduction, sufficient dimension reduction, and robust M estimators for dimension reduction. In addition, regularized regression estimators based on these reduced dimension spaces are provided, ranging from classical principal component regression to sparse partial robust M regression. The package also includes a variety of pre-processing utilities, such as generalized spatial signs, as well as dedicated plotting functionality and cross-validation utilities. Furthermore, direpack has been designed to be consistent with the scikit-learn API, making it easy to incorporate the included estimators into statistical and/or machine learning pipelines.

The World Economic Forum (WEF) is set to take place in Davos, Switzerland from May 22-25, 2023, and will bring together over 3,000 leaders from government, business, civil society, and academia to discuss global issues. The meeting will be attended by more than 20 government leaders and heads of state, including UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson, South African President Cyril Ramaphosa, and Nigerian President Muhammadu Buhari. Unlike previous years, protests against the WEF are expected to be muted. Anti-globalization campaigners have called off a demonstration planned for the weekend. The Brazilian city of Porto Alegre will host the rival World Social Forum, timed to run in parallel with the WEF's more high-profile event in Davos.

Continuous-time quantum walks have proven to be a highly versatile framework for the development of various quantum algorithms. The running time of these algorithms is often characterized by the quantum hitting time, which represents the time required for the quantum walk to locate a vertex of interest with a high probability. In this article, we present refined upper bounds for the quantum hitting time that can be applied to several ctqw-based quantum algorithms. Specifically, we utilize our techniques to enhance the hitting time upper bound for the glued-trees problem from $o(n^5)$ to $o(n^2\log n)$. Moreover, our methods also lead to an exponential improvement in the dependence on precision of the continuous-time quantum walk-based algorithm for finding a marked node on any ergodic, reversible Markov chain, as demonstrated by Chakraborty et al.

Green Day's "Boulevard of Broken Dreams" is currently at number two on Billboard's hot 100 chart, and tops its digital chart. Download sales are expected to be incorporated into the UK singles chart later this year. Digital sales in the US are already used to compile Billboard's hot digital sales chart. They will now be tallied with sales of physical singles and airplay information to make up its new hot 100 chart. Its second new chart - the pop 100 - also combines airplay, digital, and physical sales, but confines its airplay information to US radio stations that play chart music.

Iraq is inviting bids for two telephone licenses, aiming to significantly boost nationwide coverage over the next decade. The Ministry of Communications has invited local, Arab, and international companies to submit their bids. The winning bidder will partner with the Iraqi Telecommunications and Post Company (ITPC) to install and operate a fixed phone network, providing voice, fax, and internet services. The goal is to increase Iraq's very low telephone service penetration rate from approximately 4.5% to around 25% within the next 10 years, as well as develop a highly visible and dynamic telecommunications sector. The details of the bidding and tender process will be published on the ministry's website on February 9th. Additionally, the ministry is planning a roadshow for investors in Amman, Jordan to provide further information and facilitate investment opportunities.

Entanglement is a crucial resource for quantum information processing. A widely used tool for detecting entanglement is entanglement witness, which measures the witness operator and is guaranteed to be positive for separable states and can be negative for entangled states. However, as the number of parties involved in the system increases, it becomes increasingly challenging to develop an efficient entanglement witness for general multipartite entangled states. For $n$-partite Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger (GHZ)-like states, the most robust witness scheme requires $n+1$ local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/2$ white noise. As a comparison, the most efficient witness for GHZ-like states requires only two local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/3$ white noise.

Hands down my favorite first-person shooter of all time. Run-and-gun rambos need not apply. Recoil and ballistics are realistic. Single hits to the chest, neck, or head from rifle caliber weapons almost always result in death, unless it just grazes you. Rounds drop over distance and short, controlled bursts are required if you wish to remain on target. There is no magical ammunition pool. If you reload halfway through your magazine all the time, then you will have a pouch full of half-full mags. You can't run forever. If you sprint for a while, your aim will suffer as you gasp heavily until you have caught your breath. Rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a window sill to steady your aim. Accuracy suffers when standing.

An electron is typically regarded as possessing only one form of kinetic energy, but might it exhibit additional types due to its spin, charge, or interactions with other excited electrons? In one-dimensional (1D) systems, the physics of interacting electrons is effectively described at low energies by the Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid (TLL) model, though experimental observations beyond this linear regime remain scarce. Here, we present measurements of many-body modes in 1D gated wires using tunneling spectroscopy. Our results reveal two distinct Fermi seas at high energies, linked to spin and charge excitations, along with the appearance of three supplementary 1D 'replica' modes that intensify as the wire length decreases. By adjusting the degree of 1D inter-subband screening, we modulate the effective interaction strength in the wires by more than 45%.

This paper shows that the eccentric debris rings observed around stars Fomalhaut and HD 202628 are narrower than expected in the standard eccentric planet perturbation scenario, also known as the "pericenter glow." In this scenario, an initially circular and narrow belt of planetesimals at semi-major axis $a$ becomes eccentric due to secular perturbations from an eccentric planet, resulting in a belt of width $2ae_f$. However, in a minor modification of this scenario, narrower belts can arise if the planetesimals are initially eccentric, which could result from earlier planet perturbations during the gas-rich protoplanetary disc phase. Alternatively, a primordial eccentricity could be caused by disc instabilities that increase the disc eccentricity without the need for any planets.

Weight pruning is widely recognized as a straightforward and effective method to reduce redundancy in deep neural networks (DNNs), thereby achieving acceleration on various platforms. However, most pruning techniques essentially involve trade-offs between model accuracy and regularity, leading to impaired inference accuracy and limited on-device acceleration performance. To address this issue, we introduce a new sparsity dimension, called pattern-based sparsity, which includes both pattern and connectivity sparsity, making it both highly accurate and hardware-friendly. With carefully designed patterns, the proposed pruning consistently achieves unprecedented accuracy enhancement and better feature extraction capabilities across different DNN structures and datasets. Our pattern-aware pruning framework also simultaneously performs pattern library extraction, pattern selection, pattern and connectivity pruning, and weight training. Our approach to the new pattern-based sparsity naturally fits into compiler optimization for highly efficient DNN execution on mobile platforms.

Yukos is pursuing legal action to challenge the sale of its main asset and will return to a U.S. court on Wednesday to seek sanctions against Baikal Finance Group, the obscure firm behind the purchase. Yukos has announced its intention to sue Baikal and other parties involved in the $20 billion sale of Yuganskneftegas for damages. The company's U.S. legal team will also seek to freeze Baikal's assets after the Russian government disregarded a U.S. court order last week that attempted to halt the sale.  

The origins of Baikal and its motives for acquiring the unit remain uncertain. Russian media reports suggest that Baikal—which secured Yuganskneftegas for $9.4 billion (261 billion roubles, £4.8 billion) in a state-backed auction on Sunday—has close ties to Surgutneftegas, Russia’s fourth-largest oil producer.

In the depths of my being, an indelible mark remains, a testament to the profound impact you've had on my life. Though our paths may not have led to a lifelong commitment, or even a formal union, the love I harbored for you was unwavering and all-consuming. To this day, the echoes of that love continue to reverberate within my heart, a poignant reminder of the fleeting yet unforgettable moments we shared. The memories of our stolen nights, our bodies entwined as one, our souls suspended in the velvety darkness of a seaside evening, or the tender touch of a rain-kissed kiss, remain etched in my mind like a work of art. In the darkest moments, when the shards of heartache pierce my very being, it is your presence that I feel, a bittersweet reminder of the love that once burned bright within me, and still endures, a flame that flickers with every beat of my heart, a love letter to the memories we created, and the piece of you that will forever be a part of me.

Two twin domes, two radically contrasting design philosophies. One is constructed from countless steel components, the other woven from a single silk thread. One is synthetic, the other natural. One imposes itself upon the environment, the other emerges from it. One is designed for nature, the other is nature’s own design.  

Michelangelo once remarked that within raw marble, he glimpsed a figure yearning to break free. His chisel was his sole instrument. Yet living beings are not carved—they evolve. Within our most fundamental units of life, our cells, lies the complete blueprint for every function and replication.  

Tools, too, leave their mark. Since the dawn of the Industrial Revolution, the realm of design has been shaped by the demands of manufacturing and mass production.

Summer television used to be a wasteland filled with reruns, but now two exciting new shows have captured my attention. I've been watching both shows religiously over the past week. One of them, "Blow Out," features an unexpectedly flamboyant concept with hardly any visible LGBTQIA+ representation. It follows hairstylist Jonathan Antin, who is aiming to open a second branch of his widely successful West Hollywood salon, this time in Beverly Hills. Ordinarily, I wouldn't be interested at all. However, while flipping through channels, I happened upon Jonathan, who, with a mix of vanity and monotony, gazed at an empty space on Rodeo Drive and declared, "I am going to build here the greatest salon in the history of the world ever!" Strangely, I was hooked. Not since "Rocco" have I found a character this compelling on television, despite their apparent foolishness.

These are indeed grim economic times, fellow entrepreneurs, and it's a grim reality. However, I'd like to lift your spirits with a remarkable, albeit lesser-known, commercial success story from the past 20 years, one that rivals the achievements of Microsoft or Google in its own unique way. The organized crime industry has demonstrated remarkable resilience in bucking the current recession. I'm referring to the global shadow economy, or mcmafia, which has grown exponentially over the past two decades, now accounting for approximately 15% of the world's GDP. I'd like to explore what triggered this extraordinary growth in cross-border crime.

I encountered no performance issues at launch, and with the numerous patches, the game runs smoothly. In my opinion, this is a truly great and enjoyable game, despite the somewhat frustrating format where the free-roam city area repopulates with new enemies upon progressing through the story. I particularly enjoyed the 'dual play' segments, which allow you to play as Batman alongside another character. The option to use a controller for most of the game, while switching to mouse and keyboard for tank combat segments, was a welcome feature. Driving the Batmobile around the free-roam city, including during APC commander chases, is quite enjoyable once you master the use of brakes to skid around corners, making navigation through the city a truly enjoyable experience.

Weight pruning is recognized as a simple yet effective technique for eliminating redundancy in deep neural networks (DNNs), offering the potential for acceleration across multiple platforms. However, many pruning methods compromise between model accuracy and regularity, often hindering inference accuracy and limiting acceleration performance on devices. To address this issue, we introduce a novel sparsity dimension: pattern-based sparsity, which includes both pattern and connectivity sparsity, ensuring high accuracy and hardware compatibility. By using meticulously crafted patterns, our pruning method consistently enhances accuracy and improves feature extraction across various DNN architectures and datasets. Additionally, our pattern-aware pruning framework enables simultaneous library extraction, pattern selection, pattern and connectivity pruning, and weight training. This pattern-based sparsity approach is seamlessly integrated into compiler optimization, enabling highly efficient DNN execution on mobile platforms.

Ballymena sprinter Paul Brizzel will be among eight of Ireland's European indoor hopefuls competing in this weekend's AAA's championships in Sheffield. US-based Alistair Cragg and Mark Carroll are the only Irish athletes selected so far for the Europeans who will not run in Sheffield. Brizzel will defend his 200m title in the British trials. In-form James McIlroy will hope to confirm his place in the British team for Madrid by winning the 800m title. McIlroy has been in tremendous form on the European circuit in recent weeks, establishing himself as one of the fastest 800m runners in the world this winter, and already seems assured of a place in Madrid. Corkman Mark Carroll confirmed midweek that he would join Cragg in the European championships, with Carroll currently ranked number three in the world 3000m rankings, while Cragg occupies top spot.

New drink limit 'would cut toll'—more lives than previously thought could be saved by cutting drink-drive limits by a third, a report says. University College London research claims cutting the limit from 80mg of alcohol per 100ml of blood to 50mg would save 65 lives a year. The findings are being published by the Parliamentary Advisory Council for Transport Safety as MPs are due to vote on the government's road safety bill. The bill includes changes to speeding fines but not to the drink-drive limit. The research, carried out by transport expert Professor Richard Allsop, says cutting the limit would also lead to 230 fewer serious injuries on the UK's roads compared with 2003. He estimates that this would save the country £119m a year. A previous estimate, contained in a 1998 Department of Transport document, suggested such a move could save 50 lives a year.

I've been overcome with an overwhelming sense of exhaustion for the past two days. I've been plagued by debilitating fatigue, feeling as though I'm running on empty. Last night, I returned home and collapsed onto my bed, only stirring when my mother's voice pierced the darkness around 8 30 pm. As I slowly came to, I was disoriented and disoriented, struggling to discern the time or date. For a few disorienting moments, it was just my mother's gentle voice in the darkness, and I was grappling with the harsh reality. I find myself wondering why I'm feeling so drained... The morning brought a welcome respite, with a gentle drizzle falling from the sky. Although I'm told it's been raining continuously for the past three days in Calcutta.

I had the opportunity to experience EU at a later stage, having purchased it on sale, which also included EW. As a result, I was fortunate enough to avoid the initial issues that some players encountered. Despite its challenging early stages, I thoroughly enjoyed the game, finding it to be a truly rewarding experience when executed correctly. In contrast, my experience with XCOM2 has been overwhelmingly positive. The game runs smoothly on medium settings, even on my relatively outdated GTX560 with 2GB of VRAM. Initially, the game defaulted to high settings, resulting in some minor performance issues, but reducing the settings to medium resolved the problem entirely. It's possible that I've simply been fortunate in avoiding the issues that have plagued some players, but it's difficult not to notice that many of the negative reviews seem to stem from an unrealistic sense of entitlement.

On October 4, 1957, the world watched in awe and fear as the Soviet Union launched Sputnik, the world's first man-made satellite, into space. This small metal ball, smaller than two feet in diameter, ignited a space race between the U.S. and the U.S.S.R. that would last for eighteen years and transform the world as we know it. Sputnik was not the first piece of human technology to enter space, however. That superlative belongs to the V-2 rocket used by Germany in missile attacks against Allied cities as a last-ditch effort in the final years of World War II. Although it was not very effective, both the U.S. and the U.S.S.R. captured the technology and scientists responsible for its development and began using them for their own projects.

The concept of bioprinting, also known as bioprinting technology or biomanufacturing, is a relatively new field that involves the use of 3D printing techniques to create living tissue structures. The goal of bioprinting is to create functional, three-dimensional tissue structures that can be used to replace or repair damaged organs in the human body.
The idea behind bioprinting is to use 3D printing techniques to create a scaffold or matrix of biological material, such as collagen or gelatin, that can be used to support the growth of cells. The cells are then introduced to the scaffold and allowed to grow and differentiate, eventually forming a fully functional organ.
Bioprinting has the potential to revolutionize the field of organ transplantation by providing a virtually unlimited supply of organs for transplantation. Currently, there is a severe shortage of donor organs available for transplantation, resulting in long waiting lists and many people dying while waiting for a transplant. Bioprinting could potentially overcome this problem by allowing for the creation of custom-made organs that can be grown to order.
Bioprinting is still in its infancy, and there are many technical and ethical challenges that must be overcome before it

Without warning, an overwhelming fatigue has consumed me for the past two days—a deep, bone-weary exhaustion. Yesterday evening, I returned home and collapsed into sleep, only rousing when my mother called me at half past eight. Upon waking in the pitch-black room, disoriented and struggling to grasp reality, her voice was the only anchor in those fleeting moments of confusion. I can't fathom why I feel so utterly drained...  

The morning brought pleasant weather—a light drizzle as I arrived. Meanwhile, I hear Calcutta has endured three straight days of rain.

Even nature's most reviled creatures possess important secrets, but who would welcome a swarm of cockroaches approaching them? Yet, one of the greatest differences between natural and human technologies lies in robustness. Robust systems are stable in complex and novel environments. Notably, cockroaches can self-stabilize while running over rough terrain. When we equipped them with a jet pack or subjected them to a perturbation like an earthquake, we found that their exquisitely tuned legs enable self-stabilization without requiring any brainpower. They can traverse complex terrain like grass without issue, and remain unstabilized. We discovered a new behavior where, due to their shape, they automatically roll onto their side to navigate this artificial grass obstacle. Robust systems can perform multiple tasks with the same architecture. Here, we've uncovered a new behavior.

Israeli club looks to Africa for talent, with four African players, including Zimbabwean goalkeeper Energy Murambadoro, set to play for Israeli club Hapoel Bnei Sakhnin in the UEFA Cup. As the first Arab side to compete in European competition, Bnei Sakhnin will face English Premiership side Newcastle United in the first round. Zimbabwe's national team goalkeeper, Murambadoro, who gained recognition at the African Nations Cup finals in Tunisia, contributed to Bnei Sakhnin's 6-1 victory over Albania's Partizani Tirana in the previous round. Following a brief stint with South African club Hellenic, Murambadoro recently relocated to Israel. Bnei Sakhnin, based in Sakhnin near Haifa, won the Israeli Cup final last season and is known for promoting peace and co-operation within Israel, with a strong ethos to match.

The game has its pros and cons. On the plus side, the graphics are commendable. However, there are some significant drawbacks. One major issue is the numerous bugs, particularly in the environment, which can cause frustration when you get stuck or your shots hit an invisible wall despite having a clear line of sight on the enemy. Another issue is the handholding/babysitting nature of the game, which can be annoying. The AI is also quite dumb, making it easy for enemies to locate you when you're spotted. The enemy's accuracy is almost godlike, which can be frustrating, similar to the first Ghost Warrior. Additionally, the checkpoints and save system are subpar. The story is also underwhelming, and I found it difficult to care about it. Unless it's 90% off, I wouldn't recommend it. Even then, playing this game feels like an extremely boring chore, and I only play it to finish it to move on to the next game in my library. Considering the time I wasted on the first Ghost Warrior, which was equally underwhelming, I probably won't play this game until the end.

The weak gravity conjecture (WGC) demands the existence of superextremal particles in any consistent quantum theory of gravity. The standard lore is that these particles are introduced to ensure that extremal black holes are either unstable or marginally stable, but it is not clear what is wrong if this doesn't happen. This note shows that, for a generic Einstein quantum theory of gravity in AdS, exact stability of extremal black branes is in tension with rigorously proven quantum information theorems about entanglement entropy. Avoiding the contradiction leads to a nonperturbative version of the WGC, which reduces to the usual statement at weak coupling. The argument is general, and it does not rely on either supersymmetry or a particular UV completion, assuming only the validity of Einsteinian gravity, effective field theory, and holography.

Scott Richter, the self-proclaimed "spam king" and founder of optinrealbig.com, is seeking bankruptcy protection as the company, which has been battling multiple legal battles, including a high-profile lawsuit against Microsoft, faces the possibility of being forced to cease operations. Optinrealbig was listed as the third largest spammer in the world by junk mail watchdog Spamhaus and was sued in December 2003 for sending millions of spam messages that violated anti-spam laws. The company's legal troubles began when it was accused of sending billions of spam messages through 514 compromised net addresses in 35 countries. Microsoft and New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer brought the lawsuit, alleging that Mr. Richter and his accomplices sent billions of spam messages that violated anti-spam laws. The company's decision to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection would allow it to resolve its legal issues while continuing to operate.

A damn fine AAA FPS shooter - that's the bottom line. It looks, sounds, and plays great, with great FPS mechanics, a nice story, cool characters, flawless voice acting, and a badass soundtrack. Not for the faint of heart, though - some of those cutscenes were pretty darn cruel and vivid. But when a game is overall so great, it's easier to spot the flaws, and here they are as I saw them: - no SLI support - what the heck? I had to put the game away until I upgraded my card, as it ran too poorly on my old dual-GPU GTX 590. Make sure you have a fairly decent single-GPU card. - Stupidly small obstacles are often unclimbable.

Undertale transcends the sum of its parts to create something truly remarkable. The solid retro graphics and pleasant music come together to form breathtaking and impactful moments. The script masterfully navigates a wide range of emotions, from comedic to dramatic to heartbreaking and hopeful, often simultaneously, while cleverly subverting RPG tropes. Without even delving into the world, characters, story, and gameplay, there's already so much to explore. But to put it simply: if you're an RPG fan, play this game. If you enjoy video games, play this game. If you have seven free hours to spare, play this game – it's an experience you won't regret.

Online reviews provide product evaluations for customers to make informed decisions. Unfortunately, the evaluations can be manipulated by professional spammers using fake reviews ("spams") that have evolved to evade detection. Spamming strategies are difficult to capture, as they can adapt quickly over time, vary across spammers and target products, and remain unknown in most cases. Moreover, existing detectors focus on detection accuracy, which is not well-aligned with the goal of maintaining the trustworthiness of product reviews. To address the challenges, we propose a minimax game where spammers and spam detectors compete with each other on their practical goals, which are not solely based on detection accuracy. Nash equilibria of the game lead to stable detectors that are agnostic to any mixed detection strategies.

The direpack package aims to provide a comprehensive set of modern statistical dimension reduction techniques as a single, consistent package in Python. The package includes three categories of dimension reduction methods: projection pursuit-based dimension reduction, sufficient dimension reduction, and robust m estimators for dimension reduction. Additionally, regularized regression estimators based on these reduced dimension spaces are provided, ranging from classical principal component regression to sparse partial robust m regression. The package also includes a set of classical and robust preprocessing utilities, such as generalized spatial signs, as well as dedicated plotting functionality and cross-validation utilities. Furthermore, direpack has been designed to be compatible with the scikit-learn API, allowing the estimators to seamlessly integrate into statistical and/or machine learning pipelines.

The economic future of Britain will be the central focus of Labour's election campaign, Chancellor Gordon Brown has announced. He spoke following the cabinet's final meeting at No 10 before the anticipated election announcement. Brown asserted that voters would recognize Labour's role in achieving stability and growth, promising to maintain these achievements. Meanwhile, the Tories unveiled their strategies to combat "yob culture," and the Liberal Democrats provided further details on their proposals to reform council tax. Earlier, the Archbishop of Canterbury sent a letter to all three parties, urging them not to leverage people's fears during the election. In this open letter, he appealed to the parties to refrain from turning the election into a contest over who can most effectively frighten voters with concerns about terrorism, asylum, and crime.

Remind me, what did I write about before this? Or, a continuation in the 'Is this a date?' saga. So, after the weird lunch, my confidence was shaken. I forgot to tell you guys an important piece of the story. Before Pat shows up to lunch, Dustin starts talking about what a nice guy Pat is and how he's trying to find a girlfriend for him. I'm listening and nodding, realizing that we're talking more about finding Dustin's best friend a girl than discussing whatever it is you talk about with a stranger over pink lemonade. I excused myself to the bathroom, looked in the mirror, and silently asked myself: Am I being pawned off to the other guy? Is this what happens now?

Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in Josephson junctions presents an intriguing means of combining anomalous Hall and Josephson physics in a single device. We investigate theoretically how the superposition of both effects impacts the transport properties of superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. Transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of Cooper pairs through the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface generates sizable transverse Hall supercurrents, which we refer to as anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. We generalize the Furusaki-Tsukada formula, which was initially established to quantify usual (tunneling) Josephson current flows, to evaluate the transverse current components and demonstrate that their amplitudes are widely adjustable by means of the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. As a clear spectroscopic fingerprint of Josephson junctions, well-localized subgap bound states form around the interface.

In the digital marketplace, online reviews serve as a crucial resource for consumers seeking informed purchasing decisions. However, the integrity of these evaluations is often compromised by the proliferation of fake reviews, also known as "spams," orchestrated by sophisticated spammers who continually adapt and refine their tactics to evade detection. The ephemeral nature of these spamming strategies, which can shift rapidly over time, vary across spammers and targeted products, and often remain unknown, poses significant challenges to their capture. Moreover, traditional detection methods tend to prioritize accuracy over the ultimate goal of safeguarding the trustworthiness of product evaluations. To overcome these hurdles, we propose a novel approach based on a minimax game framework, where spammers and spam detectors engage in a competitive dynamic, driven by practical objectives that transcend mere detection accuracy. The Nash equilibria emerging from this game yield robust detectors that are resilient to diverse detection strategies, thereby ensuring the long-term reliability of online reviews.

As I delve into the third installment of the trilogy, I will address the unpleasant aspects first. I strongly dislike pre-qualifiers, as they often hinder the narrative's forward momentum, rather than propelling it forward (this is a common issue in pre-quals). While they may provide additional story context, the combat mechanics feel clumsy and lack the finesse of Arkham City's more refined approach. As of the time of writing this recommendation (October 26, 2013), a game-breaking bug persists, which prevents a key side mission from ever being completed (the main story remains unaffected). Some gadgets are inconveniently mapped to a hotkey, requiring an arduous journey across the keyboard to access them. Although it may seem minor, this issue can potentially disrupt combat and even break your combo.

Two twin domes, two radically opposed design cultures. One is made of thousands of steel parts, the other of a single silk thread. One is synthetic, the other organic. One is imposed on the environment, the other creates it. One is designed for nature, the other is designed by her. Michelangelo said that when he looked at raw marble, he saw a figure struggling to be free. The chisel was Michelangelo's only tool. But living things are not chiseled. They grow. And in our smallest units of life, our cells, we carry all the information that's required for every other cell to function and to replicate. Tools also have consequences. At least since the industrial revolution, the world of design has been dominated by the rigors of manufacturing and mass production.

Mefin Davies, the 32-year-old hooker who played a pivotal role in Wales' RBS Six Nations victory over England, is expected to remain with Gloucester despite reported interest from the Neath-Swansea Ospreys. Sources close to the matter indicate that while the Ospreys have expressed interest in securing Davies' services, the player is inclined to stay with the English side. Currently on a one-year contract at Kingsholm, Davies has demonstrated his value to the Zurich Premiership team, and it is likely that he will be offered a new deal for the upcoming season. Davies' future was left uncertain following the demise of the Celtic Warriors region last summer, which led to him signing a semi-professional contract with Neath RFC. Although he was afforded match time with the Ospreys at the behest of the Wales management, Davies had previously expressed his disappointment with the way he was treated, making his decision to stay with Gloucester a more appealing option.

We investigate the transport properties and superconducting proximity effect in nsn junctions composed of a time-reversal symmetry-broken Weyl semimetal (WSM) coupled to an $s$-wave superconductor. Our results demonstrate that both the differential conductance and induced pairing amplitudes exhibit a pronounced dependence on the angle between the junction's real-space orientation and the momentum-space axis separating the Weyl nodes. We further analyze the impact of a chiral chemical potential—representing the electron population imbalance between Weyl nodes of opposite chirality—on the junction's transport behavior. Notably, we detect a net spin polarization of Cooper pairs generated through Andreev reflection in both WSM regions. This polarization is oppositely oriented in the two regions and exhibits strong sensitivity to both the chirality imbalance and excitation energy.

Arnesen has denied any rift with Santini, Tottenham's sporting director, and has dismissed reports that he resigned due to a clash of personalities at White Hart Lane. According to Arnesen, the reports are 'absolutely not true', and he has spoken with Santini recently, who attributed his departure to his own personal problems. 'It's not true,' Arnesen told BBC Radio 5 Live. 'There's only one thing that made him resign, and that's his own personal problems.' Arnesen refused to comment on the specific issues that led to Santini's departure after just 13 games in charge. He did, however, acknowledge that Santini had never publicly disclosed the reasons behind his decision, but emphasized that he respected the situation. 'I think we should respect it,' Arnesen said.

Menstruation. A natural biological process, long shrouded in secrecy and stigma, has been transformed into a hushed conversation. Despite the fact that over 800 million women worldwide are menstruating at any given time, the topic remains a source of discomfort and embarrassment. As I speak, women are silently enduring the physical and emotional toll of their periods, often hiding their discomfort with a sense of shame. From my own experience and that of those around me, I can attest to the exhaustion that comes with navigating this natural yet taboo aspect of womanhood. The constant need to be discreet, the struggle to maintain a sense of normalcy despite the pain and discomfort, and the isolation that can result from feeling like an outcast are all too familiar. It is time for this stigma to be broken, for menstruation to be recognized as a natural and necessary part of life, and for women to be afforded the respect and dignity they deserve.

This game was a delightful surprise. I often come across rave reviews of certain products that are often misleading. In this case, the reviews reflect the fun of this charming little game. It's an intriguing blend between a classic 2D platformer and a MOBA, and it pulls it off with style. I found myself enjoying characters that I wouldn't typically enjoy in other MOBA games. I had expected this trait to continue, but decided to give a melee tank a try anyway. I was pleasantly surprised with the outcome and ended up trying out more as they were unlocked from playing games. The art style and interface is quaintly charming. As such, I now use one of the characters as my avatar and a wallpaper from creating a card set for the game.

I have two questions for you: whose shoulders do you stand on, and what do you stand for? These are the questions I always begin my poetry workshops with, as I believe they help students connect with the art form on a deeper level. At times, poetry can seem like a stagnant art form associated with older white men, as if they were born with a predetermined fate, like Benjamin Button. To help my students find their own voice, I ask them these questions and then share my own answers. I am the daughter of black writers, who are descended from freedom fighters who broke free from their chains and changed the world. They call me. I repeat these words as a mantra before every poetry performance, a ritual that helps me center myself. In fact, I was doing just that in the corner, preparing myself for this moment.

Magic fans, be warned... Wizards seems hesitant to create a truly engaging digital game. At its heart, Magic: The Gathering is a social experience—a game meant to be shared with friends (or frenemies), filled with laughter, playful banter, and game-changing plays. Unfortunately, Wizards of the Coast appears to have lost sight of this. If you opt to play with friends, you miss out on earning gold, which is crucial in-game. Gold is so vital that they’ve even imposed a daily cap—understandable, since it unlocks nearly everything. But brace yourself for a tedious, solitary grind in 1v1 matches against AI or silent strangers. If you’re playing for free, expect to spend roughly four hours daily for over a month, alone, just to unlock the cards.

Mom called me around 8:30 last night. When I woke up this morning, it was still dark, and I hadn't turned on the lights, so I couldn't tell what time or date it was. For a few seconds, all I could hear was my mom's voice in the darkness, and I was trying to make sense of the situation. It's nice weather today, isn't it? I heard it was raining in Calcutta for the past three days.

Prince, crowned the 'top music earner' in 2004, earned more than any other pop star, surpassing Madonna and Elton John, according to US magazine Rolling Stone's annual list. The singer banked $56.5 million (£30.4 million) from concerts, album, and publishing sales with his Musicology tour and album. He outearned Madonna, who took second place, earning $54.9 million (£29.5 million) while embarking on her global re-invention tour. Veterans Simon and Garfunkel were in 10th place, their comeback tour helping them earn $24.9 million (£13.4 million) last year. "Prince returned to center stage after a decade in the commercial wilderness," the magazine reported. His 2004 tour took in $90.3 million (£48.5 million) in ticket sales, and he sold 1.9 million copies of his latest album, Musicology.

I'm fairly certain that my sisters had a bit too much to drink today, given that it was Pride in San Diego. I know them well enough to recognize that when they're in the mood for drinking, they tend to indulge wholeheartedly. It's one trait that I didn't inherit from our family, the Buenviajes - a strong inclination towards drinking. While I do enjoy a drink on rare occasions, those instances are few and far between. With only a month left before Ed's departure, I think the reality of the situation is starting to sink in for all of us, including Ed. To be honest, it's been a harsh reality for me for quite some time now. My main concern at the moment is that Ed will be able to be present for Bailey's upcoming surgery. We've been closely monitoring Bailey's condition, and unfortunately, there's noticeable swelling that we're keeping a close eye on.

To the Moon" is far more than just a game—it's an extraordinary journey, and one of the most remarkable experiences I've ever had. Did you find Telltale's "The Walking Dead" moving? Well, prepare yourself, because it doesn't compare to "To the Moon!" Never before have I felt so beautifully melancholic and fulfilled by a fictional narrative. This masterpiece, in my view, represents the apex of storytelling, paired with a soundtrack that is both exquisitely charming and perfectly complementary. Yes, the controls can be a bit tricky, and the gameplay may not be its strongest aspect, but the narrative is so compelling that it didn't bother me in the slightest. Moreover, the humor in the game is exceptional and seamlessly blends with the story's dignified essence. And let's not forget—the Doctor Who references are abundant!

Yes, this is a nostalgic journey for thirty-somethings who remember (especially in the UK) watching the ever-irritating Timmy Mallett just to catch the five-minute Transformers cartoon. The voice actors for Optimus, Megatron, Bumblebee, and many others have returned and are instantly recognizable. The distinctive cartoon transformation sound is present. Graphically, the cel-shaded presentation is perfect and very reminiscent of Generation One. Beyond that, it's actually a very fun game. The combat mechanics are extremely well done. Transforming mid-fight often becomes necessary and is seamlessly woven into combos, making it quite distinct from High Moon's excellent War for/Fall of Cybertron games. Another distinction is the heavy focus on melee combat, although some enemies (e.g. Seekers) require switching to a ranged blaster.
